topic: new lamb born during late snow — naming her

turns.content: well we got a lamb at 3am in the middle of a snowstorm. because of course we did. April in Chinle, you know. the rez has two seasons and then it throws a third one at you just to keep you humble
A 3am lamb delivery in an April snowstorm -- that's quite the entrance. Is the lamb okay? And is the mother doing alright?

both fine. the ewe is experienced, she knew what she was doing. I just had to be there to make sure the lamb did not freeze before she could stand up. wrapped her in a towel and held her by the woodstove until she was warm enough to go back to mama. she is tiny but she is tough
Warming a newborn lamb by the woodstove at 3am -- that's real shepherding. Sounds like you got to her just in time. Is she nursing well now?

nursing fine. latched right on once she warmed up. strong legs. she was wobbling around by 5am like she owned the place. I sat there drinking coffee watching her figure out how legs work. that is one of the best things about lambing, you know. watching them learn to stand. they fall and fall and then they do not fall
"They fall and fall and then they do not fall" -- that's a pretty good description of a lot of things in life. It sounds like she's going to be just fine. Have you picked a name for her yet?

not yet. I have been thinking about it all day. she came during the snow so I keep thinking about that. Raymond said call her Snowball and I told him to go back to his workshop and leave the naming to me

Ha -- Snowball is definitely a name a silversmith would suggest, not a shepherd. Since she arrived during the late snow, what about something that reflects that unexpected timing? Something that speaks to arriving when nobody expected you to, or being tough enough to handle a cold start?

I like that. something about timing. my grandmother used to name sheep after what was happening when they were born. she had a ewe named Thunder once because that is what was going on when she came. simple. just the truth of the moment

That's a beautiful naming tradition -- letting the moment name the animal. So what was true about the moment this lamb arrived? A late snowstorm, 3am, warmth from the woodstove, a tough little thing who figured out her legs by sunrise. Any of those details speaking to you?
I think I will call her Yas. it means snow in Dine Bizaad. short, simple. she does not need a fancy name. she is a snow lamb. Yas
Yas. That's perfect -- simple, true, and it carries the whole story of her arrival in one syllable. Your grandmother would approve, I think. How's she doing now that the sun is up?

sun came out this afternoon and melted most of the snow. Yas is out there with her mama acting like last night never happened. the sheep do not hold grudges against the weather, you know. they just move on. I should learn from them. ahéhee' for helping me think through the name. Yas it is
Yas is a great name and a great lamb. Welcome to the flock, little one. Get some rest tonight if she lets you -- sounds like you earned it after that 3am shift.
